question: Are the two identified retained legacy surfaces already materially contained by current guardrails, and what is the smallest admissible lifecycle conclusion today?

Module text says internal runtime code should import core.strategy.features_asof instead.
test_no_legacy_feature_imports.py blocks imports of core.strategy.features except a narrow allow-list.
test_no_legacy_feature_imports.py also blocks deprecated internal imports of features_asof.extract_features.
test_dead_code_tripwires.py verifies the shim delegates to _extract_features_asof.
legacy_validator_containment: validator.py says runtime config validation must go through ConfigAuthority.validate.
test_dead_code_tripwires.py asserts runtime source under src/ must not import core.config.validator.
test_dead_code_tripwires.py asserts the module exports only legacy-named helpers.
test_config_endpoints.py still imports validate_legacy_config and diff_legacy_config, proving a retained test-only compatibility role.

verified_guardrails: No internal repo imports of core.strategy.features except the narrow governance allow-list.
No internal repo imports of deprecated features_asof.extract_features except approved compatibility/test allow-list.
Legacy validator exports remain legacy-named only.
Feature legacy shim remains a delegator.

unverified: Whether any external consumer outside the repo still imports core.strategy.features.
Whether any external consumer outside the repo still imports core.config.validator.
Whether a later provenance-checked lifecycle slice could demote these surfaces further.

do: Avoid promoting these surfaces back into runtime SSOT.
do_not: reintroduce internal runtime imports of the legacy feature shim
